uses: large charm granting +1 skills, experience, stats, and all resists
demand_drivers: demand is constant across all characters
supply_notes: dropped by Uber Diablo (D Clone) — spawns when enough Stone of Jordan are sold
supply is uneven — D Clone walks infrequently
duped Annihilus is a historical concern

common_quote_units: Jah Rune or Ber Rune (perfect/high rolls)
segment_sensitivity: ladder vs non-ladder: price and supply differ significantly
perfect rolls (20/20/10) trade at massive premium
known_model_risks: price varies enormously by roll quality
raw trade data aggregates all rolls — VWAP is misleading without roll classifier
low-roll Annihilus may trade near Ist; perfect roll can trade for multiple Jah/Ber
classifying roll quality from listing text requires NLP or structured listing parsing
Traderie may not reliably expose roll data in the API response

traderie.notes: Needs evaluation: does Traderie expose roll data? If not, per-roll pricing is infeasible from this source.

diablo2_io.notes: Not yet evaluated. May have better structured listing data.

reddit.notes: Use for demand, sentiment, confusion points, source discovery, and item-profile updates.

d2jsp.notes: FG prices often listed per-roll range on d2jsp.

rmt_sites.notes: External cash-market signal only. Do not present as a recommendation to buy or sell.

research_notes.note: Initial draft profile. Annihilus is a textbook roll-sensitive item. Raw VWAP across all rolls is not useful. Needs per-roll model or roll classification before pricing.

open_questions: Does the Traderie API expose roll data (attributes, resistances, experience) for Annihilus listings?
Can roll quality be classified from listing title/body text?
Should Annihilus be excluded from pricing until roll-aware modeling is possible?
What is the current per-roll price distribution?
